pi_items.source: Cancer.gov, DrugBank, FDA approval records confirm Olaparib is a real PARP inhibitor for cancer treatment
Mayo Clinic, StatPearls, DrugBank confirm Diltiazem is a real non-dihydropyridine calcium channel blocker
Liverpool HEP Drug Interactions website and Cancer iChart app documentation confirm the Traffic Light system uses Red, Amber, Yellow, Green, and Grey - NOT Orange

pi_items.verified_value: Olaparib-Diltiazem interaction is documented and real
pi_items.source: Cancer-druginteractions.org (Radboud/Liverpool), FDA prescribing information, medical literature confirm a clinically significant interaction

pi_items.verified_value: Amber severity (Potential clinically significant interaction requiring monitoring and dose adjustment)
pi_items.source: Cancer drug interactions database classifies as 'Potential Interaction' requiring 50% dose reduction of olaparib if coadministration necessary

pi_items.verified_value: Diltiazem inhibits CYP3A4/2D6; Olaparib is primarily metabolized by CYP3A4/5, leading to increased olaparib exposure when combined
pi_items.source: Multiple pharmacokinetic studies, FDA prescribing information, DrugBank confirm CYP3A4-mediated interaction

pi_items.verified_value: If coadministration necessary, reduce olaparib dose by 50% (to 150mg tablet or 200mg capsule twice daily) and monitor for toxicity
pi_items.source: FDA prescribing information, cancer-druginteractions.org, clinical management guidelines
Google Play Store listing confirmed, App developed by Liverpool Drug Interactions Group and Radboud UMC

pi_items.metadata_value: Task operates on Liverpool Cancer iChart app
pi_items.verified_value: Data from Cancer Drug Interactions Database (Radboud UMC/University of Liverpool, offline as of Sept 2023 but app contains cached data)
pi_items.source: cancer-druginteractions.org, app store listings, University of Liverpool announcements

privileged_info_summary: Olaparib is a real PARP inhibitor cancer drug approved by FDA for ovarian, breast, pancreatic and prostate cancer. Diltiazem is a real calcium channel blocker used as cardiac medication. These drugs have a documented clinically significant interaction: diltiazem (a moderate CYP3A4 inhibitor) increases olaparib exposure, requiring 50% dose reduction of olaparib and close monitoring for toxicity if coadministration is necessary. The Liverpool Cancer iChart is a real application using a Traffic Light color system (Red/Amber/Yellow/Green/Grey) to classify drug interactions, with this interaction classified as Amber severity (requires caution, monitoring, and dose adjustment). The task's metadata list of expected colors incorrectly includes 'Orange' which is not part of the actual Liverpool system.
